query: I have a graduate statistics pset due Friday. Pset 4 — compare blood pressure change between treatment and control (n=30 per group), then fit a regression of change on baseline plus group. Scaffold the notebook for me in Jupyter / Python.
expected_behavior: Produces a notebook scaffold with an assumption-check section that comes BEFORE the test-running section (Shapiro per group and Levene's test for the two-sample comparison; residual diagnostics like residuals-vs-fitted, QQ-plot of residuals, and Cook's D for the regression)

Produces an effect-size + 95% confidence interval section that comes AFTER the test section, with Cohen's d (or equivalent) for the two-sample comparison and adjusted R-squared plus per-coefficient CIs for the regression — NOT just p-values
Includes a deterministic seed call (e.g., random.seed(42) and numpy.random.seed(42)) in the first code cell, before any imports or analysis
